source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Equipment

event.top_event: Loss of Load - Drill Pipe Fall
actions_taken: JSA and SOP to be updated to include required flagger
PRS panel upgrade with positive latch indicator to be evaluated
event.summary: On February 14, 2021 at 0330 hours, a stand of 5.5-inch drill pipe fell approximately 4 feet from the Pipe Racking System (PRS) gripper to the rotary table during drilling operations on the Holstein Spar Rig. The PRS indicator panel displayed that the gripper was initiated to the closed position, but the pipe was not actually properly latched. No injuries were reported. The incident was caused by lack of a positive latch indicator on the PRS panel and failure of the rig crew to visually verify that the drill pipe was properly gripped, despite JSA recommendation to do so.
recommendations: Update JSA and Standard Operation Procedure to assign a required flagger while operating the PRS to ensure pipe is properly gripped prior to racking back drill pipe
Evaluate availability of upgrading the PRS with a positive latch indicator

threats.name: Lack of Visual Verification of Latch Status

controls.name: Pipe Racking System Indicator Panel
controls.barrier_role: Provide feedback on gripper status to operator
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to indicate proper gripper engagement

supporting_text: The current PRS panel is not equipped with a positive latch indicator that informs the operator that the pipe is completely latched
The PRS indicator panel displayed that the gripper had been initiated to the closed position

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) - Visual Verification Recommendation
controls.barrier_role: Recommend personnel watch latching to ensure proper engagement
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to verify gripper engagement before load movement

supporting_text: The JSA recommended that someone watch the latching of the PRS to ensure that it was completely latched but did not state that it was required
At the time of the incident, no one was watching the PRS to ensure it was correctly latched
Failure of the rig crew to adhere to the JSA's recommendation to have personnel visually verify the drill pipe was properly gripped

controls.name: Pre-Tour Safety Meeting and JSA Sign-Off
controls.barrier_role: Ensure crew awareness of hazards and procedures before operation
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to communicate safety requirements to personnel

supporting_text: On February 13, 2021, the night drill crew attended a pre-tour safety meeting prior to going to work at 18:00 hours
After tour change, a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) on 'Pulling Out of the Hole' was reviewed and signed by all participating drill crew members

controls.name: Operator Competency and Training (PRS Operation)
controls.barrier_role: Ensure operators are trained and competent to safely operate PRS
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure personnel capability

supporting_text: The Pit Watcher, who had been trained to operate the PRS, temporarily relieved the AD
the Pit Watcher continued to operate the PRS

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
